highlights.text: My general lean though is to defer to the trend.
As I’ve discussed at length however, it is easy to say something like “buy a dip” but knowing when and where is another story.
Fortunately, insitutions only buy dips in one way: Via my core setup - the Failed Breakdown.
In the morning we went elevator down 70+ points down to 6791.

I wrote last Friday February 13th at 4pm: “If this is the case, the obvious entry is to wait for 6795 to defend (doesn’t need to be exact) then recover today’s 6808 daily low.” Last Tuesday morning ES flushed down to 6791, recovered 6808, and we ripped hard to 6860+.

I wrote on Thursday at 4pm: “The very obvious trade here is the Failed Breakdown of today’s 6848 daily low.” Around 9:30AM on Friday morning we swept the Thursday daily low by half a point (down to 6847.25), recovered, and ripped all day.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.

I wrote on Thursday at 4pm that the target for this rally was “Targets are 6884, dip, then 6898, 6913, 6925-30, then 6954 which is a big magnet.” ES again wasted no time on this, getting to 6925-30 by 10:05AM and topped out Friday there for the day.
In doing so, ES lost Friday’s daily low at 6808.
I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “ES is rangebound now 6849 to 6925, with 6894 and 6870 being pivots inside it.

post_title: For 1 Week SPX Has Been Rangebound. Is It Ready To Leave? Feb 24 Plan
